statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.GroupingSetsTarget (GroupKey, ProductCount) SELECT COALESCE(Color, 'all'), COUNT(*) FROM bronze.Product GROUP BY GROUPING SETS ((Color), ())

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT COALESCE(Color, 'all') AS GroupKey, COUNT(*) AS ProductCount FROM [bronze].[Product] GROUP BY GROUPING SETS ((Color), ())
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH source_product AS ( SELECT * FROM [bronze].[Product] ), grouped_data AS ( SELECT COALESCE(Color, 'all') AS GroupKey, COUNT(*) AS ProductCount FROM source_product GROUP BY GROUPING SETS ((Color), ()) ), final AS ( SELECT GroupKey, ProductCount FROM grouped_data ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both extracted and refactored SQL extract from [bronze].[Product]. Refactored uses a CTE chain but sources from the same table.

output_columns.summary: Both return GroupKey (NVARCHAR(20), derived from COALESCE(Color, 'all')) and ProductCount (INT). Column names and types match.

joins.summary: Both queries have identical join structure: single source table with no joins.

filters.summary: Both queries have no WHERE clause or filtering logic. Filter behavior is identical.

aggregation_grain.summary: Both use GROUP BY GROUPING SETS ((Color), ()) to produce per-color subtotals and a grand total. Aggregation logic is identical.
